prs.title: Add type annotations to sympy/ntheory/digits.py
prs.body:
## Description
Added type annotations to the following functions in sympy/ntheory/digits.py:
- digits()
- count_digits()
- is_palindromic()

All functions now have complete type hints for parameters and return types using modern Python typing syntax (list[int], int | None, etc.).

All functions pass mypy strict mode checks.

Issue: https://github.com/sympy/sympy/issues/28806

<!-- BEGIN RELEASE NOTES -->
* ntheory
  * Added type annotations to sympy/ntheory/digits.py functions
<!-- END RELEASE NOTES -->

prs.title: Fix: show_linprog error when A_eq parameter is None
prs.body:
Fixes the SympifyError raised by show_linprog when called without A_eq parameter.

## Changes
- Added default None values to function parameters
- Imported symbols where Dummy is imported
- Added comprehensive error handling for None parameters

## Fixes
Closes #29382

<!-- BEGIN RELEASE NOTES -->
* solvers
  * Fixed show_linprog error when A_eq parameter is None
<!-- END RELEASE NOTES -->

prs.title: feat: Add getSize and getSizeWithHeaders support
prs.body:
Thanks for submitting a pull request! We appreciate you spending the time to work on these changes. Please provide enough information so that others can review your pull request. The three fields below are mandatory.

## Summary:

Explain the **motivation** for making this change. What existing problem does the pull request solve?

Example: This PR improves cache handling for FastImage by implementing proper invalidation when source URLs change with the same cache key. This solves the issue where stale images would continue to display after content updates.

## Changelog:

Help reviewers and the release process by writing your own changelog entry.

Pick one each for the category and type tags:

[ANDROID|GENERAL|IOS|INTERNAL] [BREAKING|ADDED|CHANGED|DEPRECATED|REMOVED|FIXED|SECURITY] - Message

Example: 
- [ANDROID] [FIXED] - Fixed memory leak when rapidly changing FastImage sources

## Test Plan:

Demonstrate the code is solid. Example: The exact commands you ran and their output, screenshots / videos if the pull request changes the user interface.

Example:
- Tested image loading with PNG, JPEG, GIF formats
- Verified cache behavior with `FastImage.preload()` 
- Tested on iOS 14 and Android 10
- Command: `cd example && yarn ios/android`

prs.title: fix: Add missing keys to uk.js locale
prs.body:
## Summary
This PR adds missing translation keys to the Ukrainian locale file (`uk.js`) to match the English locale (`en.js`), addressing issue #814.

## Changes
- Added missing keys to `src/i18n/locales/uk.js` including `inherits`, `show_debug_coordinates`, and others.
- Used English values as placeholders for the missing translations to prevent application errors.

## Issues
Fixes #814

prs.title: feat: Implement Dark/Light theme toggle on Landing Page
prs.body:
## Summary
This PR implements a Dark/Light theme toggle for the Landing Page to improve accessibility and user experience, addressing issue #675.

## Changes
- **Configuration**: Enabled `darkMode: 'class'` in [tailwind.config.js](cci:7://file:///Users/akashdhardubey/Downloads/ap%20/drawdb/tailwind.config.js:0:0-0:0).
- **State Management**: Created `ThemeContext` to manage theme state and persist user preference in `localStorage`.
- **Components**:
    - Added [ThemeToggle](cci:1://file:///Users/akashdhardubey/Downloads/ap%20/drawdb/src/components/ThemeToggle.jsx:3:0-19:1) component with Sun/Moon icons.
    - Integrated the toggle into the [Navbar](cci:1://file:///Users/akashdhardubey/Downloads/ap%20/drawdb/src/components/Navbar.jsx:8:0-135:1) (responsive for both desktop and mobile).
- **Styling**: Applied `dark:` variant classes to the [LandingPage](cci:1://file:///Users/akashdhardubey/Downloads/ap%20/drawdb/src/pages/LandingPage.jsx:28:0-309:1) to ensure proper contrast and aesthetics in dark mode.

## Verification
- [x] The theme toggle works instantly when clicked.
- [x] User preference is saved to `localStorage` and persists on reload.
- [x] Defaults to system preference if no user setting is found.
- [x] Verified UI consistency in both Light and Dark modes.

## Issues
Fixes #675

prs.title: docs: add typescript example for custom instance
prs.body:
This PR adds a TypeScript example to the README demonstrating how to create a custom axios instance with properly typed request/response interceptors.

### Changes
- **Modified `README.md`**: Added a code block in the `## TypeScript` section showing `axios.create` usage with `InternalAxiosRequestConfig`.

### Motivation
Developers often struggle with properly typing interceptors when using custom axios instances. This example provides a clear, copy-pasteable pattern to follow, improving the developer experience for TypeScript users.

### Related Issues
Closes #7261

prs.title: docs: add abort controller example #7263
prs.body:
This PR adds a comprehensive example demonstrating how to cancel HTTP requests using the `AbortController` API.

### Changes
- **Added [examples/abort-controller/index.html](cci:7://file:///Users/akashdhardubey/Downloads/ap%20/examples/abort-controller/index.html:0:0-0:0)**: A frontend example showing:
  - **Single Request Cancellation**: How to cancel a specific long-running request.
  - **Search-as-you-type**: A real-world pattern where previous pending requests are cancelled when the user keeps typing, preventing race conditions.
- **Added [examples/abort-controller/server.js](cci:7://file:///Users/akashdhardubey/Downloads/ap%20/examples/abort-controller/server.js:0:0-0:0)**: A simple backend handler that simulates delayed responses to facilitate the cancellation demonstration.

### Motivation
While Axios supports `AbortController`, the `examples/` directory lacked a clear, practical demonstration of this feature. This example helps developers understand how to implement cancellation patterns correctly, which is crucial for performance and user experience in modern web applications.

### How to Test
1. Run the examples server:
   ```bash
   npm run examples

prs.title: feat: Add Button Component by akashdhar
prs.body:
### Description
This PR adds a new UI component – **Button Component** – created by **Akash Dhar Dubey**.

The component is fully responsive and self-contained, with:
- `index.html` → structure
- `css/style.css` → styling
- `js/main.js` → functionality
- `assets/images/` → optional images

### Live Demo
[View the component on Netlify](https://playful-pasca-51c6de.netlify.app/)
